data.title: German metering law blocks the PDU's billing feature in its biggest market
data.narrative: The model assumes the PDU's per-outlet metering can be sold across Germany, which is half the funnel. But in Germany any meter whose reading becomes an invoice must be certified under a separate metering law, and the entrant lacks that certification. That demotes its metering to indicative only and cuts reach in the German half, so the Year-1 number is too high.

mechanism.text: German colo billing on measured kWh needs legally-verified meters; the PDU lacks certification
Germany is half the funnel; certified rivals win the metered-billing use case
Reach is overstated; certification takes 12 to 18 months, past Year 1

data.whyMissable: The team checked product-safety and energy law but missed metering law, which only bites once the meter reading becomes an invoice.
data.falsifier: German colo specs and billing interviews showing tenant power is billed at facility feed meters, with PDU metering treated as informational only.
data.likelihoodRationale: The legal requirement is certain; the open question is how much German colo billing rides on PDU-level metering versus upstream branch meters. Evidence confirms the legal rule but suggests billing often sits at branch level, so the likelihood stays near the prior with a slight uptick.
